description_long: This is a historic, timber-framed house with a steeply pitched, gabled roof covered in weathered reddish-brown tiles. The exterior features exposed wooden beams set in a decorative pattern against infill panels, typical of Tudor or medieval English architecture. A prominent brick chimney rises from one end, and a wooden staircase leads to an upper entryway, suggesting a split-level or two-story design. The windows are multi-paned and arranged in rows, with some grouped closely together and others spaced apart, adding to the asymmetrical charm. The base of the house is constructed from stone, providing a sturdy foundation and visual contrast to the timber and brick above. The overall appearance is rustic and aged, with moss and weathering visible on the roof and walls.
description: A historic, timber-framed house with a steep gabled roof, prominent brick chimney, and decorative exposed beams. Features include a wooden staircase, multi-paned windows, and a stone foundation.
description_view_0: Front and side view showing the main entry with a wooden staircase, exposed timber framing, and several multi-paned windows.
description_view_1: Rear and side view highlighting the tall brick chimney, stone foundation, and fewer windows, with the roof's steep pitch visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying a long row of windows, extensive timber framing, and the stone base along the wall.
description_view_3: Back corner view showing the gabled end, decorative timber patterns, and the continuation of the roof and chimney.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with multi-paned windows in rows
attribute_entryway_design: Main entryway accessed by an external wooden staircase, with a simple door and no porch, featuring decorative timber framing.
